headline: ⚠ Friday 5 June was a synchronized risk-off event — HMM flipped BEAR on Nikkei, Nasdaq, S&P and Copper on the same bar. Zero Bull regimes across the universe. BoJ + Warsh's first FOMC collide 16–17 June.

instruments.name: Nikkei
instruments.note: Friday −5.5% crash bar — BoJ hike trade + global AI selloff. Macro short thesis now has HMM agreement; Supertrend not yet flipped. Failed bounce toward 66–67k into the 16 Jun BoJ is the cleanest short structure.

instruments.name: Nasdaq
instruments.note: Worst day of year, 1.8× volume, Supertrend flipped down same bar. Rallies into 30,000–30,400 are the structural fade zone if Monday confirms.

instruments.name: S&P 500
instruments.note: Closed 44 pts above the Supertrend stop — one ordinary down day flips the whole US complex. Thin equity risk premium, Q2 EPS +21.6% priced for perfection.

instruments.name: FTSE 100
instruments.note: Green on the global red day — energy/defence/miners cushion. UK stagflation: CPI 3.3% rising, BoE 8-1 hold with a hike dissent. Donchian-extreme fades only.

instruments.name: DAX
instruments.note: Flat over 200 bars. EZ inflation 3.0% vs Q1 growth 0.1% — stagflation island. No trend to trade.

instruments.name: TTF Gas
instruments.note: War premium priced; trades headlines now. EU storage 38%, Gulf LNG slumped, US exports record. Binary tails both ways — regime filter says chop.

instruments.name: Brent
instruments.note: Faded from $113 as US-Iran talks flicker. Oil's war bid stickier than gas's (Supertrend still up vs TTF down). NOTE: BZM2026 expired 30 Apr — symbol map updated to BZ1! continuous.

instruments.name: Gold
instruments.note: −23% off the $5,692 peak; Friday −3.1% on 1.6× volume as hike-pricing lifted real yields. P(Bear) rising — don't knife-catch despite RSI 33 and bullish street targets.

instruments.name: Copper
instruments.note: Bear flip on −3.8% bar. ~500kt 2026 surplus gives no cushion against a growth scare. Supertrend stop 6.15 is close — flip pending.

instruments.name: Silver
instruments.note: −44% off the $122.95 squeeze peak. Friday −6.6% on 2.5× volume = liquidation bar. Let a regime flip back confirm before touching it.

instruments.name: GBP/JPY
instruments.note: The quiet tell: sterling weak even against a weak yen — BoE's stagflation trap in one price. 16 Jun BoJ hike hits with no carry cushion left.

instruments.name: USD/JPY
instruments.note: Absorption at the 160 intervention battleground; MOF has ~2 bullets left before IMF reclassification. Buy the 157.6–158 retest or fade an intervention spike near 161.95 — fresh longs at 160.3 have bad R:R in every scenario.

instruments.name: Bitcoin
instruments.note: −51% off Oct '25 ATH. $2.3bn May ETF outflows, MicroStrategy's first sale, RSI 22 bouncing off the 59,073 Donchian low. Long-only rule + Bear regime = mechanically flat.

groups.title: US Equities — the AI trade meets a hiking Fed
groups.body: Records Tuesday, worst-day-of-year Friday: a hot jobs print lifted yields and rate-HIKE odds. Equity risk premium historically thin; FactSet's +21.6% Q2 EPS is priced for perfection. Warsh's first FOMC presser 17 June — first non-Powell podium in 8+ years, maximum communication risk. NQ Supertrend already down; ES one bad day from flipping.

groups.title: Japan — the crash bar with a policy date attached
groups.body: NKD's −5.5% Friday is the BoJ trade unwinding: hike to 1.00% ~96% priced for 16 June, real wages up four straight months, Takaichi's ¥21.3tn stimulus pushing JGBs above 2%. Tech-heavy index caught the global AI selloff AND domestic rates repricing. Short-bias thesis now has HMM agreement (P(Bear)=0.99).

groups.title: Europe — the stagflation islands
groups.body: FTSE green on the global red day; both Z and FDAX HMM-Range at ~0.9 confidence. Energy/defence/miners cushion the indices while the underlying mix is poison: UK CPI 3.3% with a BoE hike dissent, EZ inflation 3.0% vs 0.1% growth. Range-trade the Donchian extremes or stand aside.

groups.title: Energy — war premium priced, trading headlines now
groups.body: Brent $93 (off $113), TTF €48.5 (+33% y/y). EU storage 38% full, Gulf LNG slumped since March, US exports at record 573.5 bcf. Both HMM-Range ≥0.90: ceasefire = air pocket lower, Hormuz escalation = spike. Untradeable chop with binary tails — exactly what the regime filter is built to flag.

groups.title: Metals — the hawkish repricing bites
groups.body: Gold −23% off peak, Silver −44%, Copper Bear-flipped. Friday's hike-pricing lifted real yields — kryptonite for non-yielders, swamping still-bullish street targets ($5,000 gold) and the central-bank-buying floor. Oversold RSIs (33/34) will tempt; the filter says wait for the flip back.

groups.title: FX & Crypto — the quiet tells
groups.body: USD/JPY absorbing at 160 with the MOF nearly out of intervention bullets. GBP/JPY weak even against a weak yen — sterling's stagflation discount. BTC is the universe's deepest bear: −51% off ATH, ETF outflows, RSI 22. Long-only rule = mechanically flat, and that is the correct trade.

playbook: 16–17 June is a scheduled vol event across the ENTIRE universe — BoJ hike + Warsh's first FOMC in 36 hours. For a 3% daily-DD account: no-trade or quarter-size that window.
Cleanest alignment: Nikkei short — macro thesis + HMM Bear 0.99. Wait for the failed-bounce 4H trigger, stop above the bounce high, inside 1× ADR.
NQ: Supertrend + HMM aligned bearish — 30,000–30,400 is the structural fade zone if Monday confirms.
Range regimes (Z, FDAX, TTF, Brent, GBP/JPY, USD/JPY): Donchian-extreme fades with basis target, or no trade. No trend entries in 0.9-confidence ranges.
Do not knife-catch metals — gold P(Bear) rising, silver's Friday bar was a 2.5×-volume liquidation.
Weekend gap risk live: weak Friday close + two days of war headlines before Sunday reopen.
Housekeeping: BZM2026 expired 30 April — symbol map updated to NYMEX:BZ1! continuous.
